channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Allianz despliega en España un sistema de detección temprana de incendios
item.description: Allianz Seguros ha iniciado en España un proyecto piloto junto a la startup Satellites on Fire para detectar incendios forestales de forma temprana mediante inteligencia artificial y tecnología satelital.

item.title: Berkley incorpora a Víctor Manuel Reviriego como responsable de RC Profesional
item.description: Berkley España refuerza su apuesta por las líneas especializadas con la incorporación de Víctor Manuel Reviriego como nuevo responsable del ramo de Responsabilidad Civil Profesional.

item.title: Prevenir litigios empieza con una buena pericia
item.description: Joaquín Ruiz Echauri, socio de Seguros y Reaseguros de Pérez-Llorca, considera que el perito de seguros se encuentra en una posición estratégica: aporta el conocimiento técnico que necesitan tanto el tramitador como el abogado para tomar decisiones sólidas.

item.title: New EVs From Toyota, Subaru Breathe Life into Struggling Market
item.description: The reeling market for electric vehicles in the U.S. in the second quarter, thanks in part to the brand’s BZ Woodland and C-HR, both of which hit the market in recent weeks. And although total volume was still down by more than a fifth from the prior year period, it provides a key glimpse into organic demand for EVs in the U.S.

item.title: Air India Crash Probe Enters Final Stages After Cockpit Recorder, Psychology Reviews
item.description: India’s aircraft accident investigating body has prepared a cockpit voice recorder transcript, conducted a psychological autopsy and moved into the final stages of its probe into last year’s deadly Air India crash, a court filing showed. The filing did not identify whose psychological autopsy was conducted or disclose any findings on the crash of the Boeing 787 that killed 260 people shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad, India. The AAIB said it expected the remaining investigation activities to be completed within about six weeks, subject to pending “external dependencies.” A draft final report is expected around October, after which it will be circulated to relevant participating countries for comments before being finalized and published, the filing said.

item.title: The Hanover Announces CEO Succession Plan; Roche to Retire at End of 2026
item.description: Roche, president and chief executive officer, plans to retire on December 31, 2026, following a distinguished 40-year career in the insurance industry. Lavey, chief operating officer of The Hanover and president of Hanover Agency Markets, has been appointed by the board as CEO-elect and will work closely with Roche to ensure a successful transition. Roche joined The Hanover in 2006 and was appointed president and CEO in 2017.

item.title: Markel strengthens greater China marine capabilities with Shanghai risk engineer hire
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: PICC Hong Kong handed double rating upgrade, positive outlook from AM Best
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hollard Insurance promotes Tony Martiniello to head of distribution
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Cyber ILS primed for future growth if traditional capacity constraints emerge: S&P
item.description: According to a new report from rating agency S&P, these evolving capabilities are laying the groundwork for alternative capital, which could play a much larger role should traditional reinsurance capacity constraints emerge in the future. While ample traditional reinsurance capacity is keeping the cyber ILS market limited for now, the underlying risk modeling and investor confidence in the asset class continues to grow. In a recent report, the rating agency notes that cyber capital market activity has subdued in 2026, with only one new cyber catastrophe bond issued so far this year.

item.title: Howden Re appoints Jay Sogliuzzo as MD, Head of NA Marine, Energy & Terror Specialty Treaty
item.description: Reinsurance broker Howden Re has announced the appointment of Jay Sogliuzzo as Managing Director, Head of North America Marine, Energy & Terror Specialty Treaty. Bradley Maltese, CEO, UK & Global Specialties at Howden Re, said, “Our global specialty practice continues to go from strength to strength, with the Marine, Energy & Terror division in particular demonstrating increased client demand. He joins Howden Re from Gen Re, where he worked across facultative and treaty reinsurance within the Marine and Energy market.

item.title: Arch Insurance NA appoints Nora Deveau as Chief Claims Officer
item.description: Arch Insurance North America, part of Arch Capital Group Ltd., has announced the appointment of Nora Deveau as Chief Claims Officer, effective 1 August 2026. Congratulations, Nora, on the well-earned promotion.” “Having grown my career at Arch, I’m excited to lead our Claims organisation into its next chapter while staying true to the values and standards that define us,” said Deveau. Deveau succeeds Patrick Nails, who is retiring following 22 years of service at Arch Insurance.

item.title: AM Best upgrades Worldwide Re’s credit ratings
item.description: Credit rating agency AM Best has upgraded Worldwide Re’s Financial Strength Rating (FSR) to B++ (Good) from B+ (Good) and the Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating (Long-Term ICR) to “bbb” (Good) from “bbb-” (Good). According to the rating agency, the assigned ratings reflect Worldwide Re’s balance sheet strength, which it assessed as very strong, as well as its adequate operating performance, neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management (ERM). Worldwide Re’s solid capital management, geographically diversified premiums, and sustained underwriting quality and profitability are also reflected in the ratings.

item.title: Cover Genius Raises $100M in Capital From Vista Credit Partners
item.description: Cover Genius, the insurtech for embedded insurance, said on July 15 it raised $100 million in capital, backed by Vista Credit Partners, a subsidiary of Vista Equity Partners. The raise ups the business to a $1.9 billion valuation, said Cover Genius, adding that it will use the new capital to deepen enterprise partnerships with enhanced integration, scale AI capabilities, and invest in platform scalability and strategic acquisitions. Cover Genius operates a B2B2C embedded protection platform that connects 200+ partners with 50+ global insurance carriers to protect over 70 million end customers at the point of sale across travel, retail, ticketing and logistics.

item.title: Farmers Looks to Make it Easier for Consumers to Understand Insurance
item.description: Farmers Insurance has launched a new initiative designed to make insurance easier to understand and more transparent for consumers. “Farmers is introducing tools that help make insurance easier to navigate—highlighting what is and is not covered, offering coverage reviews even if your policy is with another insurer, and, in some cases, helping consumers explore alternatives when Farmers isn’t a fit for them.” Changes include the new Farmers Coverage on a Page resource, a snapshot of coverage included in select Farmers auto and home policies designed to plainly show what’s covered and what’s not. Farmers said the enterprise-wide effort reflects its continuing commitment to helping consumers better understand their options so they can make more confident coverage decisions before they need it most.

item.title: People Moves: Canopius Appoints Moore US CEO; Sogliuzzo Hired to Head Howden Re North America Marine, Energy; ACORD Solutions Announces New Board of Directors
item.description: Howden Re Appoints Sogliuzzo as Managing Director, Head of North America Marine, Energy & Terror Specialty Treaty Howden Re , the global reinsurance, capital markets and strategic advisory business of Howden, headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut, appointed Jay Sogliuzzo as managing director, head of North America marine, energy and terror specialty treaty. ACORD Solutions Group Announces New Board of Directors ACORD Solutions Group , a leading provider of digital solutions for the global insurance industry, appointed four new members to its board of directors. Sogliuzzo joins Howden Re with 14 years of underwriting experience, most recently at Gen Re, where he worked across facultative and treaty reinsurance within the marine and energy market.

item.title: Australia’s M&A rules becomes DNA of dealmaking as cross-border execution risk rises
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Stripe’s Acquisition Pace Has Accelerated In The Past Five Years, But Nothing Comes Close To Its Reported $53B PayPal Bet
item.description: tech company, public or private, of the past five years, according to Crunchbase data, trailing only a handful of larger deals including VMWare’s $61 billion purchase of Broadcom in 2022 and SpaceX ’s acquisition of AI coding platform Cursor and its parent, Anysphere , for $60 billion last month. Payments giant Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have teamed up to make an offer to buy troubled PayPal in a deal valued at more than $53 billion, Reuters reported Wednesday. Stripe’s M&A pace has also accelerated sharply since 2020, Crunchbase data shows, with 13 of its 21 acquisitions announced since then.

item.title: PG&E Says Power Shutoffs Required in California on Wildfire Risk
item.description: warned that power shutoffs will be required on Wednesday and Thursday in three California counties as dry conditions and gusty winds increase fire risks. West have had to take drastic precautions such as shutting off power during increasingly warm and dry weather to limit their risk of starting deadly fires and incurring financial liabilities. This is the second time in just about a month that PG&E has resorted to so-called public safety power shutoffs.

item.title: Split P&C Market Demands Split Renewal Strategy
item.description: Property rates are easing while casualty lines tighten, requiring insureds to tailor renewal strategies by coverage line, not market averages. When the market is hard everywhere, renewal work often collapses into damage control. No, you cannot have a single word answer to the question, "Is the insurance market soft?" The more useful question is: which part of the market, and for which account?

item.title: Hiscox appoints group chief marketing and revenue officer
item.description: Hiscox has appointed Aparna Sarin as group chief marketing and revenue officer, a newly created position on the insurer’s group executive committee. Sarin joins Hiscox from Capital One, where she most recently served as chief marketing officer for business cards and payments. In the new role, Sarin will focus on Hiscox’s retail growth strategy, including customer acquisition, product development, marketing and distribution.

item.title: Addept launches legal protection for caravan and leisure homeowners
item.description: Pitch+ expands Addept’s portfolio of legal expenses products, following the company’s recent launch of marine legal protection insurance. Addept Insurance Services launched Pitch+, a legal expenses insurance product for owners of touring caravans, static caravans, motorhomes, and park homes. Available as an add-on to a core insurance policy, Pitch+ covers legal costs related to contractual disputes, site owner disputes, criminal defense arising from ownership or use of the vehicle or home, and the recovery of uninsured losses.

item.title: US Launching AI and Cybersecurity Coordination Group, White House Says
item.description: will formally bring together AI developers and essential services providers to share information on cybersecurity vulnerabilities identified by advanced AI systems and coordinate responses, according to a White House statement, fulfilling U.S. The Trump administration set up a coordination group between leading AI developers and the essential services providers so they can share information about vulnerabilities they have discovered in their software and not duplicate efforts. The arrangement includes developers of open-source AI models, said White House cyber director Sean Cairncross.

item.title: Data Breach at India’s Largest Nuclear Power Plant
item.description: The Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant, located in the southern state of Tamil Nadu, is the largest of India’s seven nuclear plants and central to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s ambitious plans to expand the country’s atomic energy capacity. Ransomware group World Leaks has posted on the dark web a huge cache of files related to India’s largest nuclear plant, including purported blueprints of parts of its facilities and supplier details — information it labeled as coming from Reliance Group. The data breach could pose a “serious” risk to the safety of the plant, says Nickolas Roth, a senior director at the Nuclear Threat Initiative, which advises governments and benchmarks countries’ preparedness on nuclear security.

item.title: AI Adoption Continues, But 70% Want More Training: SMB Survey
item.description: “Closing the gap between AI adoption and capability will be critical for Main Street success.” SMBs Find AI Training Wherever They Can Get It Despite high adoption rates and strong comfort levels (86% of SMBs said they are somewhat to extremely comfortable using AI), seven in ten say they need more, or significantly more, training to use AI productively. small businesses has risen to 66%, up 11% from a year ago, even as 70% of owners admit to needing more training to use the technology effectively, according to AI-enabled marketing platform Thryv. Despite a skills gap, small businesses are experiencing concrete benefits from AI usage, according to Thryv’s 2026 AI and Small Business Adoption Survey.

item.title: In Michigan, 100K Live at the Mercy of Aging Dams
item.description: Like more than 100,000 other Michiganders living downstream of potentially hazardous dams that are often aging, undersized or neglected, Korbecki is left to wonder how long LaBarge will hold. “Why should you be allowed to own a dam if you can’t maintain it?” Six years after dam failures near Midland prompted unsuccessful efforts to boost oversight of Michigan’s dams, safety shortcomings were laid bare in dramatic fashion again this spring as floods triggered another spate of failures and near-misses that cost taxpayers at least $5 million and likely far more. Like LaBarge, dozens of other dams deteriorate out of the public spotlight, thanks in part to federal policies that let owners shield safety records and state laws that deem Michigan dams safe when other states would consider them dangerous.

item.title: Misuse of Driver-Assistance Systems Biggest Risk to Road Safety: Survey
item.description: Motorists’ misuse of driver-assistance technology and drivers being distracted by in-vehicle features, rather than mechanical problems, are the biggest risks for road safety, according to a survey of transport specialists. Thirty percent of transport specialists surveyed cited human misunderstanding or misuse of driving assistance systems as the leading driver of mobility safety issues, while in a separate question on safety risks of user-vehicle interaction, 24% pointed to increasingly distracting in-vehicle features. In China, regulators are considering tighter oversight of advanced driver-assistance systems following a fatal crash involving a Xiaomi 1810.HK vehicle.

item.title: Zurich weighing full takeover of Indian general insurance JV: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
item.description: Methodology This tracker includes layoffs conducted by U.S.-based companies or those with a strong U.S. We source the layoffs from media reports, our own reporting, social media posts and layoffs.fyi , a crowdsourced database of tech layoffs. We recently updated our layoffs tracker to reflect the most recent round of layoffs each company has conducted.

item.title: World Bank starts work on up to $190m Nepal parametric quake catastrophe bond project
item.description: The World Bank has begun work on a proposed project to deliver a parametric earthquake catastrophe bond for Nepal, targeting US $80 million to US $190 million of fully-collateralized disaster risk financing from the capital markets for the South Asian country. The project envisages a parametric catastrophe bond that could provide around $80 million of coverage for Nepal in the event of a 1-in-20 year quake, up to $190 million for a 1-in-100 year earthquake occurring. The Nepal Catastrophe Bond Project appears to be in its nascent stages, with preparatory analysis looking at the feasibility and assessing the potential impact of delivery of a parametric earthquake cat bond for the country.

item.title: CatIQ issues initial loss estimates for June Canadian storms. Confirms final Toronto 2024 flood losses
item.description: CatIQ, Canada’s insured loss and exposure index provider, has published its initial industry loss estimates for two severe weather events that impacted the Prairies and Montreal in Canada in June 2026, while also confirming its final loss estimate for the flooding that affected Toronto and surrounding areas in mid-July 2024. CatIQ’s initial industry loss estimate for the event is CAD $728 million. CatIQ’s initial loss estimate for this event is CAD $409 million.

item.title: Capital Bay Underwriting partners with MISSION Latin America
item.description: Specialty insurance program administrator MISSION has announced that Capital Bay Underwriting, a Miami-based managing general underwriter (MGU), will be joining MISSION Latin America as the platform’s first program. Jim and I have worked with them over many years, and we’re pleased to be partnering again as part of MISSION Latin America,” said Alejandro Pedroza, President of MISSION Latin America. Formerly part of Ryan Specialty, the MGU provides financial lines facultative reinsurance solutions for both private and public corporations in the Caribbean and Latin America, having underwritten a wide range of risks across 27 countries since 2018.

item.title: Ahmed El Tabbakh to oversee Antares following passing of CEO Michael van der Straaten
item.description: Following the passing of Antares CEO Michael van der Straaten, Ahmed El Tabbakh, Group Chief Business Officer – International for QIC, the parent company of Antares, will take on the interim leadership of the Antares businesses while a new leadership structure is finalised. Antares said in a statement on Monday, ”It is with great sadness that we announce that Michael van der Straaten, chief executive of Antares, passed away unexpectedly this weekend following a short illness. In the wake of his passing, Ahmed El Tabbakh will lead the Antares businesses on an interim basis while the company finalises its longer-term leadership arrangements.

item.title: Reinsurers to remain the backbone of insurers’ ability to transfer cyber risk, says S&P
item.description: Consequently, S&P expects that “reinsurers will remain the backbone of insurers’ ability to transfer cyber risk, making the reinsurance market the primary absorber of cyber accumulation risk as cyber exposures grow in scale and interconnectedness.” Analysts added: “Ample reinsurance capacity remains available across both proportional and non-proportional structures, contributing to moderately softer reinsurance terms and continued competition among capacity providers. As increasing cyber risks and falling rates push the cyber insurance market to a critical crossroad, reinsurers will remain the backbone of the industry’s ability to transfer cyber risk. Risk flows steadily from policyholders to primary insurers and directly into the reinsurance market, where a massive share of global cyber exposure ultimately clusters.

item.title: NFP Acquires Ohio’s Total Benefit Advisors
item.description: NFP, an Aon company and leading property and casualty (P&C) broker and benefits consultant, today announced the acquisition of Total Benefits Advisors, a Cleveland, Ohio-based advisory firm specializing in retirement services and employee benefits solutions for business owners and wealth management services for high-net-worth individuals. Mark Breen, advisor and owner of Total Benefits Advisors, will join NFP as vice president, reporting to Steve Jans, Wealth Management national practice leader. Since 2009, Total Benefits Advisors has helped employers and individuals in the Cleveland area with integrated wealth management, retirement services and employee benefits solutions.

item.title: UK Steps Closer to Creating ‘Internationally Competitive’ Captive Market
item.description: Exactly a year later, on July 14, 2026 , UK regulators — the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) and the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) — laid out their plans to create “internationally competitive UK captive insurance regime.” The regulators have requested an industry consultation that will close on Oct. Insurance broker Marsh welcomed continued progress towards a UK captive insurance regime, which it described as a “significant milestone for the UK insurance market and, if delivered in a competitive and proportionate way, could strengthen the UK’s position as a credible onshore home for captive risk financing.” “Marsh is encouraged by the direction of travel set out in the consultation and the predicted outcomes,” the broker said in a press release . The UK has lagged behind many of its insurance market competitors in that it does not have a not have a tailored regime to allow the formation of captive insurers, which has led many corporations to establish their captives overseas, according to UK regulators, who hope to rectify that oversight with their plans to launch a captive insurance regime in July 2027.

item.title: FDA Sued for Allowing Unauthorized Vaping Products
item.description: The guidance states the agency would not prioritize enforcement against certain unauthorized e-cigarettes and nicotine pouch products if the companies have marketing applications under review. The group argues the policy unlawfully allows products to remain on the market without the marketing authorization required under the Tobacco Control Act, and they also said the guidance could permit thousands of unauthorized products, including flavored e-cigarettes and nicotine pouches popular with young people, to be sold indefinitely. The lawsuit also challenges the FDA’s plan to publish a list of products it does not intend to prioritize for enforcement, arguing it would facilitate sales of unauthorized products.

item.title: Coalition expands into enterprise cyber insurance with Allianz partnership
item.description: Cyber insurance provider Coalition has expanded its offering into the large corporate market, launching enterprise cyber insurance with limits of up to US$25 million . The launch follows Coalition’s recently expanded partnership with  Allianz Commercial , under which Allianz transferred its commercial cyber portfolio to Coalition and appointed the insurtech as its exclusive global cyber insurance partner across all commercial segments. As artificial intelligence amplifies attack sophistication, third-party dependencies expand attack surfaces, and regulatory requirements tighten worldwide, enterprises need confidence that they can withstand digital threats no matter how they evolve.” He added that combining Allianz’s global reach with Coalition’s claims and risk intelligence platform would give enterprise customers greater visibility into their cyber exposures.

item.title: MSIG Europe selects Clearwater Analytics to modernize investment operations
item.description: MSIG Europe , a wholly owned subsidiary of MS&AD Insurance Group , has selected Clearwater Analytics to modernize its investment operating model, consolidate investment data, and strengthen regulatory reporting across its European operations. MSIG Europe will use the platform to provide its investment and finance teams with a single reconciled view of the group’s global investment portfolio, allowing them to streamline operations and focus on strategic initiatives. The implementation will enable MSIG Europe to unify investment data across the investment lifecycle while supporting compliance with European regulatory requirements, including  Belgian GAAP, IFRS and Solvency II .

item.title: EZLynx Partners with First Connect to Bring All-in-One AMS to Member Agents
item.description: EZLynx®, an Applied Company, announced a partnership with First Connect, a digital marketplace that streamlines carrier-agent relationships and equips independent agents with the tools they need to grow. Through this partnership, First Connect members have the opportunity to receive discounted access to EZLynx’s all-in-one agency management system — with built-in automation and AI across policy management, accounting, marketing, sales, and reporting to eliminate duplicative data entry and drive agency growth. “At First Connect, we’ve removed the barriers that have traditionally held independent agencies back from accessing carriers, and now EZLynx provides our members with a powerful platform to manage the business that follows,” said Aviad Pinkovezky, CEO, First Connect.

item.title: AssuranceAmerica discloses data breach
item.description: AssuranceAmerica is notifying individuals whose personal information may have been compromised in a March cyberattack. The affected information varies by individual and may include names, contact details, auto insurance policy or account information, driver and vehicle data, claims information and driver’s license numbers. AssuranceAmerica completed its review of the affected files on June 15.

item.title: American Growth Insurance launches with $70 million
item.description: American Growth Insurance has launched with $70 million in committed equity funding from venture studio Atomic and private equity firm Rockbridge Growth Equity. “American Growth Insurance empowers local agencies to become scaled regional brokerages by embedding tailored technology tools and operational efficiency into their organizations,” the company states. American Growth Insurance completed its first agency partnership in the Mid-Atlantic region in May.

item.title: Alejandro Welter liderará la estrategia y el desarrollo de mercado de Allianz Seguros
item.description: Allianz Seguros ha nombrado a Alejandro Welter Skala nuevo director de Estrategia y Desarrollo de Mercado, un cargo que asumirá el próximo 1 de septiembre y desde el que impulsará iniciativas para el crecimiento y posicionamiento de la compañía. Además, se incorporará al Comité de Dirección y reportará directamente al consejero delegado, Veit Stutz.

item.title: Generali define mejoras en producto, operativa y digitalización
item.description: Generali ha celebrado una nueva reunión de su consejo consultivo de corredores, un foro de trabajo en el que la aseguradora y representantes de la Mediación analizan iniciativas para impulsar el desarrollo del negocio conjunto.

item.title: Anagan integra la correduría andaluza Marh Underwriting
item.description: Anagan integra la correduría andaluza Marh Underwriting

item.title: Duck Creek Acquires Send, Creating Agentic Underwriting-to-Core Platform
item.description: Duck Creek’s Agentic AI Platform will extend and accelerate Send’s underwriting orchestration capabilities by embedding insurance-native agents, governance and core system connectivity into the underwriting life cycle while giving carriers a foundation to deploy AI across underwriting with greater speed, control and business impact. Send will continue to be available as a standalone underwriting orchestration engine with integration with all core systems, while benefiting from continued investment and deeper integration with Duck Creek’s Intelligent Insurance Cloud and Agentic AI Platform for combined customers. “By bringing Send into Duck Creek, we are combining core insurance operations and underwriting orchestration in a trusted agentic AI experience that connects systems of record, decision intelligence, workflows, and action.

item.title: Corporate Venture Capital Is Splitting In Two
item.description: Two corporate venture programs shutting down inside six weeks invites speculation that corporations are retreating from venture capital, but in fact the opposite is true. According to Bain Capital , corporate investors participated in 68% of global AI deal value in 2025 — venture’s strongest funding year since 2021. Amid this strength, though, corporate venture is also quietly splitting in two, and the proof is buried inside the record numbers.

item.title: Fintech Funding Surges 23% In H1 2026 As Investors Concentrate Their Bets On AI And Financial Infrastructure
item.description: Venture funding into fintech startups climbed nearly 23% year over year in H1 2026, even as deal count fell more than 25%, Crunchbase data shows, a sign that investors are writing fewer, but much larger checks into the sector as they focus on areas such as wealth management, financial infrastructure and enterprise automation. (It’s important to note that H2 2025 marked the strongest six-month funding period for fintech startups since the second half of 2022.) Fintech funding in the first half of 2026 also topped the sector’s investment totals in 2020 and the pre-pandemic year of 2019, though they remain lower than the peak funding year of 2021 as well as 2018. “2026 marks the definitive ‘lab-i-fication’ of the modern corporation,” she noted, with some fintech platforms using their scale and steady profits to fund experimental new divisions.

item.title: Gallagher Re estimates global insured cat losses at $46bn for H1’26, 28% below 10-year average
item.description: Insured losses from natural catastrophe events across the globe in the first half of 2026 are estimated to have reached USD $46 billion, down on last year’s $84 billion and 28% below the 10-year average of $64 billion, according to reinsurance broker Gallagher Re’s H1 2026 Natural Catastrophe and Climate Report. In addition, Gallagher Re noted that economic losses also fell in the first half of 2026 to $142 billion, which is 10% below the 10-year average. However, while global catastrophe losses in H1’26 have remained below average, Gallagher Re highlights the continuing evolution of global risk, highlighted by unprecedented early summer heat in Europe, and the emergence of El Niño conditions during June.

item.title: ILS provides crucial portfolio diversification ahead of Super El Niño: VP Bank’s Allgäuer
item.description: Whilst it appears that a super El Niño could be on the horizon, a new commentary from VP Bank AG authored by Senior Investment Strategist Bernhard Allgäuer indicates that insurance-linked securities (ILS) can provide portfolio diversification as their returns are driven by natural disasters, rather than financial market turmoil, making them largely uncorrelated with traditional asset classes. “Currently, probability forecasts from the US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) suggest that a super El Niño could be on the horizon from September 2026. Importantly, Allgäuer emphasises how ILS can provide diversification, even during exceptional market events.
